mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses the geography, peoples, and history of Southeast Asia from Myanmar to the Philippines (A-B).
British colonial rule in Burma and Malaya, the Anglo-Burmese Wars, and British power over Malay sultanates are central to A.1.
Multiple European imperial enterprises in Southeast Asia -- British, French, Dutch, Spanish, and Portuguese -- shaped every part of outline A-B.
The effects of European colonialism on Southeast Asian societies and the destruction of traditional institutions connect to outline A-B.
Nascent independence movements across Southeast Asia -- including the Philippine Revolution and Vietnamese nationalism -- are central to B.1.b.iii.
The rise of nationalist movements in the Philippines (19th century) and other Southeast Asian nations under colonial rule shaped the politics of B.1.b.ii.
Theravada Buddhism in Siam and the destruction of the church-state relationship in Burma under British rule connect to A.1.a.ii and A.3.
Islamic societies of the Indonesian Archipelago and the Malay sultanates are central to A.1.b and B.2.
The Dutch East India Company, the Culture System in Java, and colonial economic exploitation illuminate outline B.2.
Portuguese and Spanish exploration and commercial activities in Southeast Asia initiated the transformations described in B.1.a.
Missionary activities in the Philippines and Vietnam, where Portuguese and French missionaries played significant roles, connect to A.2.a and B.1.b.ii.
Anglo-French activity in Southeast Asia, territorial acquisitions, and diplomatic maneuvering among European powers shaped outline A.2-A.3.
French colonial rule in Indochina -- protectorates in Annam, Tonkin, Cambodia, and Laos -- is central to outline A.2.
